name: com.dmytroudovychenko.tracking
description:
A production-grade, in-process event-tracking SDK for Unity. A tiny public API (Tracker.Init / SendMessage / SendMapAsync) sits over a non-blocking delivery pipeline, so tracking calls never block the main thread and never throw into your game code.

Pipeline: a thread-safe bounded queue (configurable DropOldest / RejectNew overflow) feeds a background batching dispatcher, which hands batches to a pluggable transport. Reliability is built in: retries with exponential backoff + jitter, a circuit breaker, a dead-letter sink, connectivity-awareness (offline hold then flush), durable persistence with a lifecycle snapshot on pause/quit, at-least-once delivery with idempotency keys, metrics, a logging hook, and a runtime privacy opt-out.

Testable by design: dependency-injection seams (ITransport / IEventStore / IClock / IDelayer / IConnectivity) make the whole pipeline deterministic, with no real network, disk, or wall-clock in the default EditMode test suite.

Sample: a 'Basic Usage' Canvas demo (init targets, valid/error sends, live metric counters) ships with the package in a hidden Samples~ folder, so it does NOT appear in the Project window. To use it, open Window > Package Manager, select this package, and under 'Samples' click Import — Unity copies it into Assets/Samples/.

samples.description: Canvas demo: init targets, valid/error sends, and live metric counters.
